checks.finding: design.md:28-59 Architecture mermaid diagram states top-level component boundaries and integration with existing components (real loop suites, existing release job via needs); design.md:9-18 Technical Summary narrates the integration; design.md:63-72 Components table and design.md:100-107 Layer Specifications table give technology choices per layer, with explicit N/A+rationale where a layer (frontend/UX) does not apply to this CLI/CI-only feature. Not a one-sentence description.

Built a directed graph from the Architecture mermaid edges (design.md:28-59): BV->LC, BV->LI, BVG->FX->STUB, RY->LG, RY->RJ, LG->RJ(needs), LG->LC2, LG->LI2, RLG->RY, RA->BVG, RA->RLG. No cycle exists in this graph.

design.md:135-151 Data Plan states 'Data Entities: none', 'Existing Data Affected: none', and 'Migration Strategy: none' with concrete rationale for each sub-field (this is the section remedied after the round-1 Major finding; confirmed intact and unchanged in round 3).

design.md:153-308 API / Contract Plan documents the exact scripts/bump-version.sh insertion point and planned code block, the fixture-repo test technique, the release.yml loop-gate job's planned YAML, and the text-marker test technique for release.yml -- the CLI/CI-appropriate coverage for a feature with no HTTP surface, consistent with ux-spec.md:1-11 and frontend-spec.md:1-6 both stating no browser/frontend application exists.

design.md:409-418 Security Boundaries table names B1-B4, states Auth/Authz Mechanism per boundary, addresses Data Classification (no PII; synthetic/internal source only, consistent with requirements.md:328-337's own boundary table), and states OWASP Concerns per boundary, cross-referencing security-spec.md#trust-boundaries' fuller OWASP Mapping table (security-spec.md:75-83). Confirmed the round-2 remedy is present: Constraint Compliance rows for fail-closed pre-mutation ordering/zero-mutation (design.md:448) and the cross-host named-SKIP degradation note (design.md:451).

SKIP: Feature Type is 'release-gate wiring (two additive test suites + edits to two already-existing release surfaces)'. ux-spec.md:1-11 and frontend-spec.md:1-6 both state 'N/A -- no change: ... no browser or frontend application.' No frontend/backend split exists to check consistency against.

design.md:310-342 Test Strategy covers a positive/negative test pairing rationale (item 1: AC-001 green vs AC-002/AC-003 red), a RED-demonstrable structural proof (item 2: AC-009 negative-branch canary), concrete planned commands ('bash tests/run-all.sh', 'pwsh tests/run-all.ps1', item 4) plus the 3-OS CI matrix and release-only ubuntu-latest job as authoritative evidence, and self-registration verification (item 5). acceptance-tests.md maps every AC-001..014 to a named TEST-001..014.

Every node named in the Architecture diagram (BV, LC, LI, BVG, FX, STUB, RY, LG, RJ, LC2, LI2, RLG, RA) is defined in the Components table (design.md:63-72), the 'Real surfaces exercised READ-ONLY' note (design.md:74-78), or the API/Contract Plan subsections (design.md:196-308).

design.md:125-133 ADR Change Log states no new ADR is needed with rationale (reuses epic-159-pillar-a2's established fixture-copy and text-marker precedents; introduces no new vocabulary, schema, or architectural pattern). The two 'New decision' entries in Design Decisions (design.md:364-391) are scoping choices following existing precedent, not architectural departures requiring an ADR.
